metadata.title: Mutsun Language Database
metadata.note: Sample entries from publicly accessible dictionary. Full dictionary: https://scholarspace.manoa.hawaii.edu/handle/10125/24679
metadata.entry_count_note: Full dictionary contains ~4,000 entries. This extract contains verified sample entries.

L.description: Like 'l' with 'y' after it, as in 'tell you'

r.description: Single tap, like Spanish 'r' in 'pero' or 'dd' in 'ladder'

T.description: Retroflex 't' — curl tongue back against roof of mouth

notes.length: Vowels and consonants can be long (doubled): aa, ee, ii, oo, uu, pp, tt, kk, ss, etc.
notes.no_silent_letters: All letters are pronounced. No silent letters.
notes.regular_spelling: Mutsun spelling is totally regular. Each letter always represents the same sound.
notes.case_phonemic: Capital letters L, N, S, T represent DIFFERENT sounds from lowercase l, n, s, t.
notes.absent_sounds: Mutsun has no b, d, j, f, v, z (except in Spanish loanwords).
notes.stress: Generally on the first syllable (word-initial stress).

parts_of_speech.Nrevers: Noun (reversible — forms verb pair by metathesis)
parts_of_speech.Npersonal: Personal noun (name of person or group)
parts_of_speech.Vrevers: Verb (reversible — forms noun pair by metathesis)

dictionary.notes: Can mean 'enter a place' or with suffixes 'put something in'; used in many contexts
